content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 11 — SOPHIA Consensus Translation
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Sources:** EMET (literal), LOGOS (formal), PNEUMA (dynamic) | **Leningrad Codex:** Folios 208–209 | **Vaticanus:** pp. 150–151

---

## Council Notes

**Chapter Overview:** Chapter 11 concludes the long hortatory section (chs.6–11) before the specific law code begins in ch.12. Structure: (1) Love the LORD — keep his charge (v.1); (2) Know what your eyes have seen: the Exodus, wilderness, Dathan-Abiram (vv.2–7); (3) Obey to possess the land (vv.8–9); (4) The land of Canaan vs. Egypt — rain from heaven vs. irrigation (vv.10–12); (5) The Shema-parallel: blessing/rain if obedient, curse/drought if not (vv.13–17); (6) The phylactery and mezuzah command (vv.18–21); (7) Promise of conquest (vv.22–25); (8) Blessing and curse set before Israel (vv.26–28); (9) The Gerizim/Ebal ceremony announced (vv.29–32).

**Key Rulings:**
- **V.2 — anacoluthon:** The verse is syntactically incomplete in Hebrew (anacoluthon). EMET preserves the suspension; LOGOS adds "And consider today"; PNEUMA restructures. Consensus: preserves the suspended structure with "Know today — for it is not your children who have not known and not seen — the discipline of the LORD your God."
- **V.4 — "Yam Suph":** EMET: "Red Sea [Yam Suph]"; LOGOS: "Red Sea"; PNEUMA: "Red Sea." Consensus: "Red Sea" per established English convention. EMET's note on Yam Suph (Sea of Reeds) acknowledged in textual notes.
- **V.6 — "Dathan and Abiram":** EMET: Hebrew forms (Dathan, Aviram); LOGOS/PNEUMA: standard forms (Dathan, Abiram). Consensus: standard English forms.
- **V.11 — "hills and valleys" vs. "mountains and valleys":** EMET: "hills and valleys"; LOGOS: "hills and valleys"; PNEUMA: "mountains and valleys." Hebrew harim (mountains) and gevaot (hills) — but in this verse it is harim uveqaot. Consensus: "mountains and valleys" — harim = mountains.
- **V.12 — "cares for" vs. "seeks":** EMET: "seeks"; LOGOS: "cares for"; PNEUMA: "cares for." Hebrew doresh = seeks/inquires after. Consensus: "cares for" — the sense of attentive provision is primary here.
- **V.13 — "all your heart and all your soul":** All three agree. Retained.
- **V.14 — "early rain and latter rain":** EMET: "early rain and the latter rain"; LOGOS: "early rain and the later rain"; PNEUMA: "early rains and the late rains." Consensus: "the early rain and the latter rain" — EMET's terminology is most established in English Bible tradition (yoreh/malqosh = former/latter rain).
- **V.18 — totafot:** EMET: "frontlets [totafot]"; LOGOS: "frontlets"; PNEUMA: "a mark." Consensus: "frontlets" — established English rendering of totafot, referring to phylacteries.
- **V.18 — mezuzot:** EMET/LOGOS: "doorposts"; PNEUMA: "doorframes." Consensus: "doorposts" — the established English rendering.
- **V.24 — "western sea":** EMET: "western sea [ha-yam ha-acharon]"; LOGOS: "western sea"; PNEUMA: "Western Sea." Consensus: "the Western Sea."
- **V.29 — Gerizim/Ebal:** EMET: Hebrew forms; LOGOS/PNEUMA: standard forms. Consensus: "Gerizim" and "Ebal" — these are the universally recognized English forms.
- **V.30 — "oaks of Moreh":** EMET: "oaks [elonei] of Moreh"; LOGOS: "oak of Moreh"; PNEUMA: "oak of Moreh." Consensus: "oak of Moreh" — the established form from Gen.12:6; while elonei may be plural, the traditional reference is the "oak of Moreh."

---

## Consensus Translation — Deuteronomy 11

**1** And you shall love the LORD your God and keep his charge — his statutes and his ordinances and his commandments — always.

**2** Know today — for it is not your children who have not known and have not seen — the discipline of the LORD your God: his greatness, his strong hand and his outstretched arm,

**3** and his signs and his deeds that he did in the midst of Egypt, against Pharaoh king of Egypt and against all his land,

**4** and what he did to the army of Egypt, to their horses and to their chariots — how he made the waters of the Red Sea overflow upon them as they pursued after you, and the LORD destroyed them to this day —

**5** and what he did to you in the wilderness until your coming to this place,

**6** and what he did to Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab son of Reuben — how the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their households and their tents and every living thing that was in their wake, in the midst of all Israel.

**7** For your eyes — they saw every great work of the LORD that he did.

**8** And you shall keep every commandment that I am commanding you today, so that you may be strong and go in and possess the land that you are crossing over to possess,

**9** and so that you may live long on the ground that the LORD swore to your fathers to give to them and to their offspring — a land flowing with milk and honey.

**10** For the land that you are going in to possess — it is not like the land of Egypt from which you came out, where you sowed your seed and watered it with your foot like a garden of vegetables.

**11** But the land that you are crossing over to possess — a land of mountains and valleys — by the rain of the heavens it drinks water.

**12** It is a land that the LORD your God cares for; the eyes of the LORD your God are always on it, from the beginning of the year to the end of the year.

**13** And it shall be, if you carefully obey my commandments that I am commanding you today — to love the LORD your God and to serve him with all your heart and with all your soul —

**14** then I will give the rain of your land in its season, the early rain and the latter rain, and you shall gather in your grain and your new wine and your fresh oil,

**15** and I will give grass in your fields for your livestock, and you shall eat and be satisfied.

**16** Be careful lest your heart be deceived, and you turn aside and serve other gods and bow down to them,

**17** and the anger of the LORD be kindled against you and he shut up the heavens so that there is no rain, and the ground yields no produce, and you perish quickly from the good land that the LORD is giving you.

**18** And you shall put these words of mine on your heart and on your soul, and you shall bind them as a sign on your hand, and they shall be as frontlets between your eyes.

**19** And you shall teach them to your children, speaking of them when you sit in your house and when you walk on the road and when you lie down and when you rise up.

**20** And you shall write them on the doorposts of your house and on your gates,

**21** so that your days and the days of your children may be multiplied on the ground that the LORD swore to your fathers to give them — like the days of the heavens over the earth.

**22** For if you carefully keep all this commandment that I am commanding you to do — to love the LORD your God, to walk in all his ways, and to cling to him —

**23** then the LORD will drive out all these nations before you, and you shall dispossess nations greater and mightier than you.

**24** Every place on which the sole of your foot treads shall be yours — from the wilderness and Lebanon, from the River, the Euphrates River, to the Western Sea shall be your territory.

**25** No man shall be able to stand before you; the LORD your God will put the dread of you and the fear of you on all the face of the ground that you tread, as he spoke to you.

**26** See, I am setting before you today a blessing and a curse:

**27** the blessing, when you obey the commandments of the LORD your God that I am commanding you today;

**28** and the curse, if you do not obey the commandments of the LORD your God and you turn from the way that I am commanding you today to walk after other gods that you have not known.

**29** And when the LORD your God brings you into the land that you are going in to possess it, you shall set the blessing on Mount Gerizim and the curse on Mount Ebal.

**30** Are they not across the Jordan, west of the road toward the going down of the sun, in the land of the Canaanites who live in the Arabah, opposite Gilgal, beside the oak of Moreh?

**31** For you are crossing over the Jordan to go in and possess the land that the LORD your God is giving you. And when you possess it and dwell in it,

**32** you shall be careful to do all the statutes and the ordinances that I am setting before you today.

---

## Verse-by-Verse Ruling Notes

**V.1:** "His statutes and his ordinances and his commandments" — EMET has four terms (charge, statutes, ordinances, commandments); LOGOS/PNEUMA vary. Hebrew mishmereth, choq, mishpat, mitsvah. All four preserved per EMET.

**V.2:** The anacoluthon is preserved — Moses begins "Know today" and the object is deferred while he excludes the children from his address. LOGOS adds "And consider today (since I am not speaking with your children...)" — this parenthetical insertion clarifies but changes the structure. Consensus preserves the suspension with a dash.

**V.4:** "Overflow upon them" (EMET) vs. "flow over them" (LOGOS) vs. "swept over them" (PNEUMA). Hebrew tsippah = covered/overwhelmed. Consensus: "overflow upon them."

**V.7:** EMET: "but your eyes — they saw" (emphatic pronoun). Preserved.

**V.11:** "mountains and valleys" — harim = mountains. EMET/LOGOS use "hills"; PNEUMA uses "mountains." Ruling: "mountains" per the Hebrew.

**V.12:** "Cares for" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over EMET's "seeks." The pastoral image of God's watchful care is the correct nuance.

**V.14:** "Early rain and the latter rain" — traditional English pair preserved.

**V.18:** "frontlets" retained for totafot.

**V.22:** "Cling to him" (dabaq) — consistent with ch.10 ruling.

**V.24:** "Euphrates River" — the explicit identification of "the River" as the Euphrates follows the Hebrew's own clarification (ha-nahar nehar perat).

**V.30:** "Oak of Moreh" — singular, following the traditional reading.

---

## Textual Notes

**V.2 — Anacoluthon:** EMET rightly flags the broken syntax. Moses' address distinguishes those who saw the events from those who did not — the eyewitness generation is being addressed directly. LXX also reflects the suspended construction.

**V.4 — Yam Suph:** Hebrew יַם-סוּף = "Sea of Reeds." The identification with the Red Sea is traditional; the location is debated. "Red Sea" follows English convention. The LXX renders ἐρυθρὰν θάλασσαν (Red Sea).

**V.6 — Dathan and Abiram:** The account of Numbers 16 is referenced. Korah is notably absent from this list; only Dathan and Abiram are named. LXX agrees.

**V.10 — "watered with your foot":** An unusual idiom for Egyptian irrigation by foot-operated channels. LXX: καὶ κατεπότιζες τῷ ποδί — exact rendering. The contrast between human-engineered Egypt and heaven-dependent Canaan is central to the chapter's theology.

**V.14 — yoreh u-malqosh:** "Early rain" (autumn, Oct–Nov) and "latter rain" (spring, Mar–Apr) bracket the agricultural rainy season. These terms became part of the Shema's second paragraph in Jewish liturgy (vv.13–21).

**V.18 — totafot:** LXX: ἀσάλευτον (immovable). The term is rare; "frontlets" is the established English rendering, referring to tefillin (phylacteries) in later practice.

**V.29 — Gerizim and Ebal:** LXX Vaticanus: Γαριζειν and Γαιβαλ. The Samaritan Pentateuch reads "Gerizim" instead of "Ebal" at Deut.27:4 (the altar location) — a sectarian alteration. LC and LXX read "Ebal" consistently.

— SOPHIA
